reasoning: The vulnerable path is the default-config public API. The NodeRSA constructor (line 3761) accepts string|Uint8Array directly and calls importKey() with no format argument. importKey() (line 3792) applies only an empty-string check (line 3793) and then calls detectAndImport() with format=undefined. detectAndImport() iterates all four FORMATS providers calling autoImport() on each; componentsFormat.autoImport() exits immediately for non-object input, so string input reaches all three PEM-based providers (opensshFormat, pkcs1Format, pkcs8Format) in sequence, executing all six vulnerable regexes. No non-default options, no schema validation (no zod/joi/ajv), no length limit, and no content sanitization exist anywhere in the path. The only guard is the empty-string check which does not mitigate the attack. A crafted ~10 KB string with a PEM BEGIN marker but no END marker (e.g., '-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----' + 'A'.repeat(10000)) is sufficient to trigger multi-second event loop stalls. The package README documents the constructor as accepting raw key strings, making attacker-controlled input the intended and primary use case. Reachability without authentication at default configuration is fully confirmed.
